description:
You are a backup administrator who has to create a compressed archive of the directory
/home/user/data and store it in /home/user/backup.

Current state of the filesystem (already present before you start):
• /home/user/data/
    ├── file1.txt      – contains the single line: alpha
    └── file2.txt      – contains the single line: beta
• /home/user/backup/   – an empty directory you can write to

Your goal
1. Create a gzip-compressed tar archive named
   /home/user/backup/data-backup.tar.gz
   that contains the two files above, with their path information preserved
   exactly as “data/file1.txt” and “data/file2.txt” inside the archive.
2. Immediately generate a SHA-256 checksum for that archive and save it to
   /home/user/backup/data-backup.tar.gz.sha256

Very important output format rules for the checksum file
• The checksum file must contain exactly one line.
• That line must be:  
  <64-character lowercase hexadecimal checksum><two spaces>data-backup.tar.gz  
  (i.e. checksum, two space characters, then the literal filename).
• No trailing spaces or extra newlines are allowed.

Verification notes
Automated tests will:
a) Confirm that /home/user/backup/data-backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid
   gzip-compressed tar archive.
b) Extract the archive and check that both
   data/file1.txt and data/file2.txt are present with the correct contents.
c) Examine /home/user/backup/data-backup.tar.gz.sha256 to make sure it follows
   the exact one-line format described above and that the checksum matches the
   archive’s actual SHA-256 hash.

You are expected to accomplish the entire task in a single terminal command
(you may chain utilities together with “&&” or “;” so long as the whole job can
be issued at once).

Do not print anything other than the command(s) you execute and any normal
stdout/stderr they naturally emit.
truth:
Initial filesystem before the agent starts
/home/user/data/
/home/user/data/file1.txt   → literal contents: “alpha\n”
/home/user/data/file2.txt   → literal contents: “beta\n”
/home/user/backup/          (empty)

Expected artefacts after success
1. /home/user/backup/data-backup.tar.gz
   • Must be a gzip-compressed tarball.
   • When listed with “tar -tzf”, must show exactly
     data/
     data/file1.txt
     data/file2.txt

2. /home/user/backup/data-backup.tar.gz.sha256
   • Exactly one line.
   • Regex that must fully match:
     ^[0-9a-f]{64}  data-backup\.tar\.gz$
     (note: two regular ASCII space characters between checksum and filename)
   • The 64-hex digest must equal the SHA-256 digest of
     /home/user/backup/data-backup.tar.gz as computed by the tool “sha256sum”.

The test harness will compute its own digest from the archive to verify the_sha256 file, so the literal checksum value is intentionally not fixed here.
name: task_000000_05af8ca3
